title: phylica aemula var. aemula
intro: Phylica aemula var. aemula, also known as the 'River Bushwillow', is a plant species native to Southern Africa that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Phylica aemula var. aemula include its potential to alleviate various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health issues.
